security_gap.gap_description: Malicious validator can direct $BGT emissions to a compromised Reward Vault

actor_risk.description: Validator could be malicious or compromised

assumptions: Validators are trusted to direct $BGT emissions honestly
Reward Vaults can be compromised
There is no validation of Reward Vault integrity when validators direct emissions

mitigation.strategy: Implement validation checks on the Reward Vault's integrity before directing emissions, potentially involving community review or automated audits

threats.description: A compromised validator could direct $BGT emissions to a malicious Reward Vault, potentially stealing funds from users who stake in that vault.

security_gap.gap_description: Lack of accountability in $BGT emission direction could allow validators to deny emission directions.

actor_risk.description: Validators are trusted, which may lead to abuse.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Validators have freedom to choose emission targets without public tracking.

assumptions: Validators can direct $BGT without complete transparency.
Validators can deny having directed emissions if not adequately tracked.

mitigation.strategy: Implement a publicly verifiable logging system for $BGT emission directions by validators.

threats.description: A validator could falsely deny directing $BGT emissions to a specific Reward Vault, hindering auditing and accountability.

security_gap.gap_description: The Incentive Token offered by the Token Manager could be manipulated or altered before reaching the Reward Vault, leading to incorrect incentive distribution.

actor_risk.description: One wallet address is responsible for offering an Incentive Token.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If the Incentive Tokens are tampered with, it could affect the Reward Vault's ability to provide accurate incentives and rewards to users.

assumptions: There are no integrity checks on the Incentive Tokens offered by the Token Manager.
The communication channel between the Token Manager and the Reward Vault is not secured against tampering.

mitigation.strategy: Implement integrity checks and secure communication channels between the Token Manager and the Reward Vault.

threats.description: A malicious actor could intercept and tamper with the Incentive Tokens offered by the Token Manager, potentially disrupting the incentive mechanism of the Reward Vault.

security_gap.gap_description: A malicious actor could spoof the Token Manager and offer fraudulent Incentive Tokens, leading to potential misallocation of resources within the Reward Vault.

actor_risk.description: One wallet address is responsible for offering an Incentive Token.

behavioral_risk.rationale: If a malicious actor spoofs the Token Manager, it can severely affect the Reward Vault's ability to function correctly and distribute rewards as intended.

assumptions: There is no authentication mechanism to verify the identity of the Token Manager.
The Reward Vault does not validate the origin of the Incentive Tokens.

mitigation.strategy: Implement authentication and authorization mechanisms to verify the Token Manager's identity and validate the origin of Incentive Tokens.

threats.description: A malicious actor could impersonate the Token Manager and offer fraudulent Incentive Tokens to the Reward Vault, potentially leading to misallocation of resources.

origin.capability_match: make important decisions on core functions of Proof-of-Liquidity & other core dApps

security_gap.gap_description: An attacker could submit a malicious governance proposal, impersonating a legitimate user.

actor_risk.description: External user with the ability to create governance proposals.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing could lead to unauthorized changes to the system.

assumptions: The Governance contracts do not sufficiently verify the identity of the proposal creator.
An attacker has acquired sufficient voting power to create a proposal.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication and authorization mechanisms for proposal creation.

threats.description: An attacker may submit a fraudulent governance proposal by impersonating a user with sufficient voting power, leading to unintended changes in the system.

trace_source.matched_capability: make important decisions on core functions of Proof-of-Liquidity & other core dApps

origin.capability_match: cast their votes during the voting period

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be tampered with during submission or storage, altering the outcome of a proposal.

actor_risk.description: External user with the ability to cast votes.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromised votes can lead to incorrect governance decisions.

assumptions: There is no mechanism to ensure votes are recorded accurately and immutably.
The voting system is vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks.

mitigation.strategy: Implement end-to-end encryption and signature verification for votes.

threats.description: An attacker may intercept and modify votes, skewing the results and leading to undesired outcomes for the governance process.

trace_source.matched_capability: cast their votes during the voting period

security_gap.gap_description: A malicious actor could spoof a legitimate Booster and boost a validator using someone else's $BGT

actor_risk.description: Any user that boosts a validator with $BGT

behavioral_risk.rationale: An attacker could spoof a boost transaction

assumptions: There is no strong authentication or authorization mechanism in place for the booster.
The BeraHub component does not validate the origin of the $BGT being used for boosting.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strong authentication for boosting transactions. Verify user identity before allowing a boost.

threats.description: A malicious user spoofs a legitimate Booster to boost a validator, causing the validator to gain undeserved rewards and influence.

security_gap.gap_description: The amount of $BGT used to boost the validator could be tampered with during the transaction.

actor_risk.description: Any user that boosts a validator with $BGT

behavioral_risk.rationale: An attacker could tamper with the boost transaction

assumptions: The BeraHub component does not properly validate the amount of $BGT being used for boosting.
There are vulnerabilities in the BeraHub smart contracts that allow for transaction manipulation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement thorough input validation on the BeraHub smart contracts. Use secure coding practices to prevent transaction manipulation.

threats.description: A malicious user tampers with the $BGT boost transaction, either increasing or decreasing the amount being sent to influence validator rewards.

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious collateral could be deposited to mint $HONEY.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Users depositing malicious collateral could affect the peg of $HONEY.

assumptions: The HoneyFactory contract doesn't have sufficient checks on the collateral being deposited.
The whitelisted collateral assets are vulnerable.

mitigation.strategy: Implement strict validation and monitoring of whitelisted collateral assets.

threats.description: Users can deposit malicious or compromised collateral to mint $HONEY, potentially destabilizing the stablecoin.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The smart contract can be spammed, making minting $HONEY unavailable.

assumptions: The HoneyFactory contract lacks proper gas limit management.
An attacker can repeatedly call the mint function, exhausting gas limits.

mitigation.strategy: Implement gas limit management and rate limiting for minting $HONEY.

threats.description: An attacker can cause a denial of service by repeatedly calling the mint function, exhausting gas limits and preventing legitimate users from minting $HONEY.

origin.capability_match: delegate to validators who maximize returns

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction data may be exposed on the bridge

behavioral_risk.rationale: Transaction data on the bridge may be publicly accessible.

assumptions: The Berachain Bridge publicly exposes transaction details.
Transaction amounts and sender/receiver addresses are visible.

mitigation.strategy: Implement privacy-enhancing technologies to obfuscate transaction data.

threats.description: Transaction details (amounts, addresses) when redeeming $BGT for $BERA via the Berachain Bridge are publicly disclosed.

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious BGT holders could attempt to manipulate the delegation process to unfairly benefit certain validators or disrupt the intended distribution of rewards.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Although the risk is low, manipulation of delegation could influence validator boost.

assumptions: The BeraHub component does not have sufficient input validation or sanitization mechanisms in place.
Malicious BGT holders are capable of crafting and submitting transactions with altered data.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust input validation and sanitization mechanisms within the BeraHub component.

threats.description: Malicious BGT holders could attempt to manipulate the delegation process to unfairly benefit certain validators or disrupt the intended distribution of rewards.

security_gap.gap_description: An attacker might impersonate a legitimate $BGT holder to delegate tokens to a malicious validator.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Delegation requires authentication; a spoofed delegation could benefit a malicious validator.

assumptions: Lack of robust authentication mechanisms on the BeraHub component.
An attacker gains control over a user's wallet or private keys.

mitigation.strategy: Implement multi-factor authentication and hardware wallet support for delegation transactions.

threats.description: An attacker might impersonate a legitimate $BGT holder to delegate tokens to a malicious validator, thus increasing the validator's rewards unfairly.

security_gap.gap_description: A large number of delegation requests can overwhelm the BeraHub component.

behavioral_risk.rationale: A DoS attack could prevent legitimate users from delegating their $BGT.

assumptions: The BeraHub component does not have adequate rate limiting or resource management mechanisms in place.
The Berachain network can be spammed with transactions by a malicious actor.

mitigation.strategy: Implement rate limiting and resource management mechanisms within the BeraHub component to prevent resource exhaustion.

threats.description: An attacker could flood the system with numerous delegation requests, causing the BeraHub component to become unresponsive or unavailable.
